## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

    the verification path, slate as separate pace if structural or out-of-scope,
    capture as itch if speculative.

    initial debug per operator).

    routing.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

content.text:
Strong question — let me read the paddock locks against the spec surface carefully.

## What's locked vs what's open

**BBAAN rev 3 "Settled decisions" (treaty-grade locks):**

| Concept | Lock status | Sufficient for spec? |
|---------|-------------|----------------------|
| Cult-verb triad (invest/divest/muster) | Named | ✓ Yes |
| Per-role split (retriever/director instances) | Named | ✓ Yes |
| Mantle stays as singleton exception | Named | ✓ Yes |
| Colophon scheme `rbw-a[rd][IDm]` with full table | Named | ✓ Yes |
| Letter-case semantic load (caps mutating, lower observation) | Named | ✓ Yes |
| Singleton-per-role invest (zero instance arg) | Behavioral | ✓ Yes |
| Producer-output: invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively | Behavioral | ✓ Yes |
| Divest-by-role: reads RBRA, REST DELETE | Behavioral | ✓ Yes |
| Divest post-delete removes production RBRA | Behavioral | ✓ Yes |
| Invest preexistence fatal | Behavioral | ✓ Yes |
| Unmake silently absorbs governor cleanup (404-tolerant) | Behavioral | ✓ Yes |
| Observation-fact convention via `<basename>.<type>` | Named | ✓ Yes |
| `RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_MUSTER` constant identities | Named | ✓ Yes |
| `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` constant identities | Named | ✓ Yes |

**What's NOT locked:**

| Open thing | Owner | Spec impact |
|------------|-------|-------------|
| Exact fact-extension constant names (RBCC names) | Mount-time decision | None at quoin level — spec describes the convention, not specific extension strings |
| Function names (`rbgg_invest_retriever` etc.) | Convention, settable at mount | Spec rarely names implementation symbols — handbook does, but that's BBAAR's territory |
| Exact assay slot path layout | Mount-time | None — spec describes "assay slot as binding producer output", not the path |

## Reading

The constraint "spec describes settled reality, not intent" exists to prevent the failure mode where spec text locks in vocabulary that the implementation ultimately rejects. With BBAAN's paddock containing a "Settled decisions" block this thorough, the verb-concept vocabulary is treaty-locked: any divergence at code-mount time would be a paddock violation, not a graceful adjustment.

The two surfaces blocked-on-BBAAN under the strict reading are actually safe to author from paddock-settled vocabulary, because **the spec talks about concepts (verb meaning, colophon shape, behavioral contract), not implementation symbols (function names, exact constant strings)**. The implementation-symbol grain is where late drift happens; the concept grain is where the paddock binds.

**Risk that remains:** if BBAAN's mount-time review surfaces a paddock-revisit (rare but possible), spec text gets a small follow-on edit. That's a much smaller cost than holding the spec sweep until BBAAN's code lands and the testing thread releases the operator's attention.

**My revised recommendation: full BBAAQ scope is authorable now**, minus the handbook (which is genuinely BBAAR's). Reslate to drop only the rbho/rbhp surface; author all four spec surfaces (BUS0 multi-fact, RBS0 cult-verb, README glossary, RBS0+RBSDE depot-identity banner) this session.

If a later mount-time decision in BBAAN forces a rename, I'll sweep here. The likelihood is low given paddock thoroughness.

Want me to draft the reslate gazette dropping handbook scope, then proceed with the four surfaces?

### cult-verb-foundation (₢BBAAN) [rough]

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; muster replaces list. Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Muster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, muster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDm]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Muster | `rbw-arm` | `rbw-adm` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (m) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Muster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_MUSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_muster_retrievers`, `rbgg_muster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent and the `rbfl_muster_*` image-muster precedent.

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Muster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for muster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Muster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).

Character is mechanical — the new shape is settled in the paddock; this pace adapts theurge to it. ## Scope

Pristine-lifecycle section restructures. BBAAJ's §2 (pristine-lifecycle-simple) and BBAAK's original §3 (pristine-lifecycle-credentialed) collapse into a single unified section containing three fused cases that share one throwaway depot for the run:

- Depot stand-up — depot levy and the rbrr field install needed to satisfy downstream preconditions.
- SA cycle — governor mantle, retriever invest, director invest, access-probes, divests of operational SAs.

Test consumes per-row observation facts via type-extension lookup. All stdout-substring assertions retire from pristine-lifecycle code. Test-side primitives reduce to tabtarget invocation, file copy (assay → role production path), `Path::exists()` on facts, and one fact-read for depot project_id.

## BBAAN sequencing implications

These flow from BBAAN's settled implementation conventions and constrain the fused-case ordering.

**Post-divest production RBRA absence.** "Divests of operational SAs" leaves no production RBRA at the role's path (BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA decision). Any subsequent fixture step that needs those credentials must re-invest first. The current SA-cycle ordering (mantle → invest → access-probes → divest) is correct as written; tear-down does not need credentials.

**Invest fatal-on-exists.** Re-invest within a single fixture run requires divest first (BBAAN's fatal-if-exists decision). The fused-case design already lands invest exactly once per role; no in-run re-invest needed.

**Governor cleanup folded into unmake.** No separate "forfeit governor" step appears in the fused case — `Depot tear-down — unmake, which absorbs governor cleanup` is the entire cleanup. The 404-tolerant unmake makes ordering robust to mid-fixture failures (an unmake after a failed mantle still cleans up cleanly).

**RBCC fact-extension constants.** BBAAN consolidates fact-extension tinder constants into one contiguous block in RBCC (migrating `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC; new retriever/director ext constants join it). Rust-side consumers — including `RBTDRP_FACT_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID` and any new fact-name imports — must point to the RBCC home. Pristine fixture is currently service-tier-broken pending BBAAM Step 5; this remap is part of the bring-back-online work for this pace.

### cult-verb-foundation (₢BBAAN) [rough]

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; muster replaces list. Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Muster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, muster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDm]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Muster | `rbw-arm` | `rbw-adm` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (m) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Muster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_MUSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_muster_retrievers`, `rbgg_muster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent and the `rbfl_muster_*` image-muster precedent.

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Muster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." # jjezs_reslate BBAAC

## Character

Creates the persistent cloud infrastructure used by all downstream phases of `rbw-tP`. Runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture (BBAAB) gates and the lifecycle exercises pass. This is the "real" mantling/investing plus the "real" canonical depot levy — the state that persists for reliquary inscribe, hallmark builds, and crucible runs.

Quota-aware: canonical depot creation is one of the daily quota draws (the other being the throwaway depot in BBAAB's depot-lifecycle case). Two GCP projects per pristine run total.

Depends on BBAAN landing (cult-verb foundation) so the new colophons are available.

## Work

Add a phase to `rbw-tP` that runs after the pristine-lifecycle fixture passes:

1. **Levy canonical depot** — `tt/rbw-dL.PayorLeviesDepot.sh`; verify GAR repository exists, worker pools provisioned. Use the canonical name (RBRR-derived), distinct from throwaway names used in BBAAB.
2. **Mantle canonical governor** — `tt/rbw-aM.PayorMantlesGovernor.sh`; deploy governor RBRA file at the configured RBRR path.
3. **Invest canonical retriever** — `rbw-arI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to retriever's production path.
4. **Invest canonical director** — `rbw-adI` colophon (BBAAN); RBRA written to assay slot, copied to director's production path.
5. **Muster + verify** — `rbw-arm` (retrievers) and `rbw-adm` (directors) emit per-role observation facts via type-as-extension naming. Governor presence verified implicitly via depot health (Mason SA HEAD probe surfaces in `rbw-dl` depot state per BBAAM Step 4 multi-fact reshape). Verification via fact-file presence and depot-state, not stdout-substring assertion.
6. **Active depot assertion** — `RBDC_*` derived constants resolve to the canonical depot.
7. **IAM propagation wait** — phase cannot exit until IAM propagation completes for all three production SAs; downstream phases depend on auth working.
8. **Discrete checkpoint UX** — phase exits with a clear "Canonical infrastructure ready. Re-run rbw-tP to proceed; this phase will detect existing canonical state and skip if no marshal-zero has occurred since." message.

Re-run discipline: when operator re-invokes `rbw-tP` after BBAAC has already created canonical infra, BBAAC detects existing canonical state (depot exists matching RBRR-derived name, RBRAs present) and skips the create steps. Note tension: the lifecycle fixture's case 1 gate (marshal-zero attestation) will still fail if RBRAs are present, so re-runs after canonical setup require running rbw-tP with case 1 skipped (e.g., resume from BBAAC onward via a phase-skip flag, or by single-case running individual lifecycle cases). Design call to be made during this pace.

Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists posture: skip-when-present logic must rely on cloud probe before any invest call, since blind invest atop existing cloud SA fatals. Per BBAAN's divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any "tear down before re-create" path must invest after divest, never invest atop existing cloud SA.

## Verification

- After phase runs: canonical depot exists, all three RBRAs present at configured paths
- `rbw-arm` and `rbw-adm` muster outputs surface all expected SAs via observation facts
- `tt/rbw-dl.PayorListsDepots.sh` shows canonical depot active
- IAM propagation has completed (subsequent reliquary inscribe + hallmark ops succeed without auth-related retries)
- Re-running rbw-tP after this phase detects existing canonical infra and skips the levy/mantle/invest/invest steps
- Quota cost recorded (one canonical depot + one throwaway depot from BBAAB = two daily quota units)
- Failed levy or mantle surfaces with operator-actionable diagnostic (quota? auth? config?)

## Out of scope

- Throwaway-named SA/depot lifecycle (BBAAB)
- Reliquary inscribe (BBAAE)
- Crucible runs (BBAAF)
- Cleanup at end-of-tier (BBAAG)
- Resolving the case-1-gate-vs-canonical-state re-run tension beyond a design call (full design lives in this pace)

6. Surface large surprises for follow-up paces (new heats if they're significant)

### Recording

Record into the pace closure:
- Wall-clock total and per-phase
- Cloud cost (depot creation, build minutes, GAR storage)
- Surprises captured + their disposition (fixed inline / spawned follow-up)

## Verification

- Cleanup phase leaves depot empty and unmade after each pristine run
- First end-to-end run completes (or fails informatively) with surprises captured
- Wall-clock and cost recorded for future capacity planning
- All four crucible fixtures green at the conclusion of the first successful run
- The bug class this heat exists for (silent first-build assumptions) is either caught or proven absent

## Out of scope

- `/rbk-prep-release` integration (Phase 6)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios (out of single-operator scope)
- Quota recovery beyond unmake-at-end-of-tier
- Restoring RBRAs after marshal-zero (manual operator step per runbook)

3. Run `rbw-tP` — full sequence; expect ~1 hour wall-clock
4. Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX — in a notch dedicated to this pace's findings
5. Fix small surprises inline within this pace (one-line bash fixes, message clarity)
6. Surface large surprises for follow-up paces (new heats if they're significant)

### Recording

Record into the pace closure:
- Wall-clock total and per-phase
- Cloud cost (depot creation, build minutes, GAR storage)
- Surprises captured + their disposition (fixed inline / spawned follow-up)

## Verification

- Cleanup phase leaves depot empty and unmade after each pristine run
- First end-to-end run completes (or fails informatively) with surprises captured
- Wall-clock and cost recorded for future capacity planning
- All four crucible fixtures green at the conclusion of the first successful run
- The bug class this heat exists for (silent first-build assumptions) is either caught or proven absent

## Out of scope

- `/rbk-prep-release` integration (Phase 6)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios (out of single-operator scope)
- Quota recovery beyond unmake-at-end-of-tier
- Restoring RBRAs after marshal-zero (manual operator step per runbook)

- Spec/banner-copy updates for the new (CLOUD_PREFIX, MONIKER) shape — owned by sibling pace ₢BBAAQ.
- `rbgp_depot_list` reshape — delivered under BBAAM Step 4 (commit `8eb44097`).
